body.title: Verify end-to-end before flipping gates — verification is part of the build budget
body.statement: A gate flips only on a runtime-observable signal: a log line, a behavioral change, a metric, a UI element, a database row, an end-to-end flow producing user-facing output. 'Code merged' and 'unit tests green in isolation' are not gate-flipping signals. Every decision and ticket budgets the end-to-end verification pass as part of the work, not as overhead to trim when time runs short. Gate here means any go-live switch: a feature flag, a ticket close, a decision status change, a dependency ungate.
body.why: A gate flipped on assertion ships hollow builds posing as done (CP1). Proof-on-close is this rule at ticket granularity; the consequence-checking gap (impl closed but never shipped) is what this rule exists to prevent at decision granularity.
